finding_results.notes: The snapshot's offending lines ('that's where the comedy lives,' 'Self-commentary is where comedy goes to die,' 'precision is everything you've got,' 'That's the engine,' 'It's the most useful thing I've ever told anyone I've worked with') had already been removed or replaced in a prior pass. One residual thesis statement remained at line 175: 'The second you start managing how you sound, it goes generic. That's how every guy with a viral clip talks once he starts explaining himself.' This generalized wisdom about 'every guy with a viral clip' violated Simone's prohibition against mentor-figure speeches and was redundant — the diagnosis was already fully delivered at line 171 ('you step outside yourself and start explaining Doug to me. I can't use that') and Doug processes the implications himself in the narrator paragraph at line 177. Trimmed line 175 to 'It’s a useful one,' Simone said.' — four words, no thesis, consistent with her low repetition tolerance and economical register. The door-scene passage (lines 288-292: 'The shampoo isn't for the set' / 'I'm writing it down because it's exact. That's different.') was already concrete, short, and leaves implications unstated. Both passages now satisfy the acceptance test: concrete scene callbacks present, no quote-card-ready abstract statements, at least one implication left for Doug to infer.

summary: Single finding addressed. Removed the last residual mentor-wisdom thesis from Simone's dialogue at line 175, where a general statement about 'every guy with a viral clip' followed an already-complete diagnosis. The trimmed line ('It's a useful one,' Simone said.) honors her style-bible register: dry, economical, says things once. The door-scene passage (lines 288-292) required no changes — already short, concrete, and implication-rich. All snapshot offenders ('that's where the comedy lives,' 'Self-commentary is where comedy goes to die,' 'precision is everything you've got,' 'That's the engine') were absent from the current chapter. Heading contract preserved. Continuity verified against sheet (Doug age 52, divorce 12 years, marriage 18 years, Roy's sandwich order, shampoo detail, art print). No new continuity contradictions introduced. Character contraction levels verified: Doug moderate, Simone high. No style-bible example lines present verbatim or near-verbatim.
